process_representation.evidence: State-of-the-art ocean general circulation model based on MITgcm with comprehensive representation of ocean dynamics, thermodynamics, sea ice, and biogeochemistry. Validated against extensive observational datasets.

spatial_resolution.evidence: LLC90 (~1° global) to LLC270 (~1/3°) grid configurations. Unstructured lat-lon-cap grid supports regional refinement but not full eddy-resolving global coverage.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Hourly to multi-decadal simulations. ECCO v4r4 covers 1992-2017 with daily to monthly output fields spanning the full ocean state.

process_coupling.evidence: Ocean-sea ice coupling is fully interactive. Coupled to atmospheric forcing fields. Ongoing work on coupling to ISSM for ice sheet-ocean interactions.

predictive_skill.evidence: Demonstrated skill in ocean state estimation, sea level prediction, and ocean heat content trends. Used in IPCC assessments and CMIP-class intercomparisons.

computational_performance.evidence: Scales well on HPC systems (NASA Pleiades). MITgcm supports MPI parallelism. GPU support under development but not yet standard.

observational_constraint.evidence: Assimilates satellite altimetry, Argo floats, XBTs, CTDs, SST, sea ice concentration, ocean bottom pressure (GRACE). Multi-source, multi-platform constraint.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: ECCO v4r4 is a fully integrated, dynamically consistent ocean reanalysis product (1992-2017). Benchmarked against independent observations and other reanalyses.

uq_attribution.evidence: Adjoint-based sensitivity analysis and formal uncertainty propagation for key state variables. Ensemble approaches used for specific studies but not routine.

vv_framework.evidence: Extensive validation against independent datasets. Participation in OceanPredict, GODAE, and CMIP intercomparisons. Systematic benchmarking documented in peer-reviewed publications.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: ML used in limited research contexts for parameterization discovery and data gap-filling. Not yet integrated into core production workflow.

mission_support.evidence: Key contributor to GRACE/GRACE-FO, SWOT, and Sentinel-6 mission formulation. OSSEs conducted for satellite mission planning. Provides synthetic observations for mission concept studies.

interoperability.evidence: Fully open source (MITgcm on GitHub). Standardized output formats (NetCDF). Active community with external contributors from MIT, UT Austin, AER, SIO.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Used by NOAA for ocean monitoring, by coastal planning agencies for sea level projections. Growing adoption but not yet core operational tool for decision-makers.

process_fidelity.evidence: Comprehensive ocean physics including mesoscale eddies, mixing, boundary currents, overturning circulation.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: Strong ocean-sea ice coupling. Atmosphere treated as external forcing. Ocean-ice sheet coupling in development.

community_recognition.evidence: Widely cited in IPCC AR5/AR6, WCRP assessments. Over 600 peer-reviewed publications using ECCO products.

extreme_events.evidence: Can represent marine heatwaves and extreme sea level events at resolved scales. Limited by resolution for coastal extremes.

obs_coverage.evidence: Assimilates 14+ observation types from multiple satellite missions and in-situ networks.

error_correction.evidence: Adjoint-based 4DVAR provides rigorous, dynamically consistent bias correction across the full state vector.

prediction_impact.evidence: Significant improvement in ocean state estimates. Forecast skill demonstrated for seasonal-to-decadal timescales.

operational_use.evidence: Products used operationally by multiple agencies. Not yet embedded in real-time operational prediction systems.

verification_level.evidence: MITgcm has rigorous code verification with automated test suites and regression testing.

ml_in_processes.evidence: Research-stage ML for parameterization and emulation. Not in production.

vvuq_ml_emulators.evidence: No formal VVUQ framework for ML components yet.

independent_validation.evidence: Extensive validation against withheld datasets, independent reanalyses, and field campaign observations.

osse_capability.evidence: Full OSSE framework used for SWOT, GRACE-FO mission design. Synthetic observation capability well-established.

da_readiness.evidence: New satellite data types can be incorporated but require significant development for each new instrument.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Directly supports multiple Decadal Survey priority areas: sea level, ocean circulation, ice-ocean interaction.

community_adoption_mission.evidence: Core tool for NASA ocean mission formulation. Used by ESA, NOAA for mission planning.

operational_usability.evidence: Products available but require expert interpretation. Not packaged for direct operational use.

regulatory_relevance.evidence: Sea level projections used in coastal planning regulations. Referenced in IPCC policy-relevant chapters.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Formal uncertainty estimates available for key variables. Communication to non-expert users needs improvement.

community_training.evidence: Regular tutorials at scientific conferences. Growing online documentation. Limited direct stakeholder engagement.

process_representation.evidence: Comprehensive ice sheet dynamics including higher-order stress balance, grounding line migration, calving, solid Earth deformation, and sea level fingerprinting. Three coupled sub-models (ice, solid Earth, sea level).

spatial_resolution.evidence: Adaptive mesh refinement from continent-scale to sub-kilometer resolution at ice stream margins. Unstructured finite element mesh supports multi-scale representation.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Sub-annual to multi-millennial simulations. Time-stepping adapted to process timescales. Some limitations in representing very short-term dynamics.

process_coupling.evidence: Fully coupled ice sheet-solid Earth-sea level system. Coupling to ECCO ocean model in development. Represents gravitational, rotational, and deformational effects.

predictive_skill.evidence: Used in ice sheet projections for IPCC AR6. Demonstrates skill in hindcasting observed ice loss but uncertainty remains large for future projections.

computational_performance.evidence: Parallel finite element solver scales on HPC. Demonstrated scaling to thousands of cores. GPU acceleration under development.

observational_constraint.evidence: Assimilates satellite velocity fields, surface elevation, gravity data (GRACE). Limited by sparse observations in basal conditions.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: Inversions for basal conditions using satellite observations. Multi-decadal reconstructions of ice sheet evolution, but not continuous reanalysis.

uq_attribution.evidence: Adjoint-based sensitivity analysis and sampling-based UQ for projections. Attribution of ice loss to atmospheric vs oceanic forcing.

vv_framework.evidence: Participation in ISMIP6, initMIP, and other international ice sheet model intercomparison projects. Rigorous benchmarking against analytical solutions.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: ML explored for emulating ice sheet response and parameter estimation. Not yet in core workflow.

mission_support.evidence: Supports GRACE/GRACE-FO and ICESat-2 mission science. Contributes to NISAR planning for ice velocity measurements.

interoperability.evidence: Open source on GitHub. Active external collaborator network (UC Irvine). Standard file formats and documented API.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Sea level projections used in coastal planning and IPCC scenarios. Growing engagement with policy community.

process_fidelity.evidence: State-of-the-art ice dynamics with higher-order stress balance, damage mechanics, and hydrology.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: Three-way coupling between ice sheet, solid Earth, and sea level. Validated coupling framework.

community_recognition.evidence: Lead contributor to ISMIP6/IPCC projections. Widely cited in cryosphere and sea level research.

extreme_events.evidence: Can simulate rapid ice loss events but limited skill in predicting timing and magnitude of ice cliff instability.

obs_coverage.evidence: Satellite velocity, elevation, and gravity observations. Limited subsurface/basal observations available.

error_correction.evidence: Adjoint-based inversion for basal drag and ice rheology. Systematic error reduction through iterative optimization.

prediction_impact.evidence: DA significantly improves initial conditions for projections. Impact on long-term predictability still being quantified.

operational_use.evidence: Not used in operational prediction systems. Products used for planning-horizon projections.

verification_level.evidence: Rigorous code verification against analytical benchmarks. Automated test suites.

ml_in_processes.evidence: Research-stage ML for emulation and parameter estimation.

vvuq_ml_emulators.evidence: No formal VVUQ for ML components.

independent_validation.evidence: Validated against independent satellite observations and field measurements. Limited by observation availability at depth.

osse_capability.evidence: Limited OSSE capability. Can generate synthetic observations but no systematic framework.

da_readiness.evidence: Can incorporate new satellite data types with moderate development effort.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Directly supports Decadal Survey priorities on ice sheets and sea level.

community_adoption_mission.evidence: Used in NASA mission science teams. Growing adoption in ESA mission planning.

operational_usability.evidence: Projections require expert interpretation. Not packaged for direct decision support use.

regulatory_relevance.evidence: Sea level projections referenced in IPCC, used in coastal planning regulations.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Probabilistic projections with uncertainty bounds. Communication framework improving.

community_training.evidence: Conference tutorials and documentation. Limited direct stakeholder engagement.

process_representation.evidence: Atmospheric transport model (GEOS-Chem) with flux inversion framework. Represents CO2 transport, biospheric and oceanic exchange, fossil fuel emissions.

spatial_resolution.evidence: Global inversions at 4°x5° to 2°x2.5°. Regional nested grids at higher resolution. Limited by atmospheric transport model resolution.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Monthly to sub-daily flux estimates. Multi-decadal flux records from satellite era.

process_coupling.evidence: Off-line coupling: atmospheric transport driven by meteorological reanalysis. Biospheric models provide prior fluxes. No interactive coupling.

predictive_skill.evidence: Demonstrates skill in reproducing observed CO2 concentrations and seasonal cycles. Used for carbon budget assessments.

computational_performance.evidence: Runs on HPC systems. Adjoint model requires significant computation. Limited GPU/cloud deployment.

observational_constraint.evidence: Assimilates OCO-2, OCO-3, GOSAT satellite CO2, surface flask networks, aircraft profiles, and in-situ tower data. Multi-source constraint.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: Multi-year flux inversions providing consistent carbon flux records. Not continuous operational reanalysis.

uq_attribution.evidence: Formal uncertainty propagation through adjoint-based framework. Attribution of CO2 fluxes to regions and processes.

vv_framework.evidence: Validated against independent observations and other inversion systems. Participates in TransCom intercomparisons.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: ML explored for flux downscaling and gap-filling. Not in core workflow.

mission_support.evidence: Key system for OCO-2/OCO-3 mission science. Supports future GeoCarb and carbon monitoring mission concepts.

interoperability.evidence: Data products publicly available. Core inversion code shared with collaborators. Not fully open source.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Products used by Global Carbon Project, national carbon inventories. Growing policy relevance for emissions verification.

process_fidelity.evidence: Captures key carbon cycle processes through transport and flux inversion. Limited by prior flux model fidelity.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: Off-line coupling limits feedback representation. Atmospheric transport prescribed from reanalysis.

community_recognition.evidence: Well-cited in carbon cycle research. Products used in Global Carbon Budget reports.

extreme_events.evidence: Can detect anomalous carbon flux events (droughts, fires) but limited temporal resolution for extreme event analysis.

obs_coverage.evidence: Assimilates multiple satellite and in-situ CO2 observation networks.

error_correction.evidence: Adjoint-based optimization corrects transport and flux biases. Bias correction for satellite retrievals included.

prediction_impact.evidence: DA significantly improves flux estimates relative to prior models.

operational_use.evidence: Not in operational prediction systems. Used for research and retrospective analysis.

verification_level.evidence: Code verification through comparison with established transport models.

independent_validation.evidence: Validated against TCCON, aircraft campaigns, and independent inversion systems.

osse_capability.evidence: OSSEs conducted for OCO-3 and GeoCarb mission design.

da_readiness.evidence: Can incorporate new CO2 satellite data with moderate development.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Supports carbon cycle priorities in Decadal Survey.

community_adoption_mission.evidence: Used by NASA OCO science teams and carbon monitoring community.

operational_usability.evidence: Products require expert interpretation for policy use.

regulatory_relevance.evidence: Growing relevance for national emissions verification and Paris Agreement stocktake.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Formal uncertainty estimates on flux products. Improving communication to policy audiences.

community_training.evidence: Scientific community engagement through publications and conferences.

process_representation.evidence: Muskingum river routing with lateral inflow from land surface models. Represents river network connectivity and flow propagation. Simplified hydraulics.

spatial_resolution.evidence: Global river network at varying resolution (HydroSHEDS-based). Represents hundreds of thousands of river reaches worldwide.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Sub-daily to monthly routing time steps. Output at 3-hourly to daily frequency for most applications.

process_coupling.evidence: Coupled to multiple land surface models (Noah-MP, CLM, VIC) through the NASA Land Information System (LIS). Off-line coupling to atmospheric forcing.

predictive_skill.evidence: Validated streamflow predictions at thousands of USGS gauging stations. Demonstrates skill for flood forecasting in limited settings.

computational_performance.evidence: Highly parallelized (namesake: Routing Application for Parallel computatIon of Discharge). Scales efficiently on HPC. Demonstrated cloud deployment.

observational_constraint.evidence: Assimilates satellite altimetry (SWOT concept), gauge data, and GRACE-derived water storage. Growing observational constraint.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: Multi-decadal streamflow records produced using reanalysis-driven land surface models. Used for water resource assessment.

uq_attribution.evidence: Limited UQ through ensemble forcing and parameter perturbation. No formal probabilistic framework.

vv_framework.evidence: Validated against USGS gauges globally. Participates in model intercomparisons. Systematic benchmarking for major river basins.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: ML explored for parameter estimation and runoff prediction. Not in core workflow.

mission_support.evidence: Key supporting model for SWOT mission. Demonstrates value of satellite hydrology observations.

interoperability.evidence: Fully open source on GitHub. Standard interfaces for coupling with land surface models. Active external community.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Used by NOAA NWS for experimental flood forecasting. Adopted by international water resource agencies.

process_fidelity.evidence: Good representation of river routing processes. Simplified relative to full hydrodynamic models.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: Well-integrated with land surface models. Off-line atmospheric coupling.

community_recognition.evidence: Well-cited in hydrology community. Used in large-scale water resource assessments.

extreme_events.evidence: Demonstrates skill for large-scale flood events. Limited by Muskingum simplifications for extreme floods.

obs_coverage.evidence: Satellite and gauge observations. SWOT will significantly expand coverage.

error_correction.evidence: Limited formal bias correction. Relies on calibration against observed discharge.

prediction_impact.evidence: DA improves streamflow estimates, particularly in gauged basins.

operational_use.evidence: Experimental operational use at NOAA NWS. Not yet core operational tool.

verification_level.evidence: Code verification through benchmark tests and analytical solutions.

independent_validation.evidence: Validated against USGS gauges and satellite altimetry.

osse_capability.evidence: OSSEs conducted for SWOT mission concept. Can generate synthetic river observations.

da_readiness.evidence: Ready for SWOT data incorporation. Framework adaptable to new observation types.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Supports surface water priorities in Decadal Survey.

community_adoption_mission.evidence: Key tool for SWOT pre-launch science team.

operational_usability.evidence: Streamflow products accessible. NOAA NWS experimental use demonstrates operational potential.

regulatory_relevance.evidence: Limited regulatory use. Potential for flood zone delineation.

community_training.evidence: Open source community, tutorials, documentation. International collaborations.

process_representation.evidence: Simplified but comprehensive terrestrial carbon cycle model. Represents photosynthesis, respiration, allocation, turnover, decomposition, and fire.

spatial_resolution.evidence: Typically 1° to 4° gridded applications. Resolution limited by computational cost of Bayesian inference at each grid cell.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Monthly to annual carbon flux estimates. Multi-decadal retrievals from satellite era.

process_coupling.evidence: Driven by meteorological forcing and satellite observations. No interactive coupling to atmospheric or hydrological models.

predictive_skill.evidence: Demonstrates skill in reproducing observed carbon stocks, fluxes, and their interannual variability. Validated against eddy covariance and inventory data.

computational_performance.evidence: MCMC inference is computationally expensive. Parallelizable across grid cells. Limited HPC optimization.

observational_constraint.evidence: Assimilates satellite LAI, biomass, soil carbon, and burned area. Multi-source constraint on terrestrial carbon cycle.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: Multi-decadal retrievals of terrestrial carbon cycle state and fluxes. Provides consistent long-term records.

uq_attribution.evidence: Bayesian MCMC framework provides full posterior distributions for all parameters and state variables. Formal uncertainty propagation and attribution.

vv_framework.evidence: Validated against FLUXNET, forest inventories, and independent satellite products. Benchmarked against other terrestrial biosphere models.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: ML explored for emulating MCMC inference. Not in core workflow.

mission_support.evidence: Supports interpretation of satellite vegetation and carbon observations. Limited direct mission formulation role.

interoperability.evidence: Code available on GitHub. Active collaboration with Stanford, UCSB, Edinburgh. Standard data formats.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Primarily research use. Carbon stock and flux products have potential for forest carbon monitoring.

process_fidelity.evidence: Captures key terrestrial carbon cycle processes. Simplified relative to full Earth system models.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: No interactive coupling. Driven by prescribed forcing.

community_recognition.evidence: Well-cited in terrestrial carbon cycle research. Growing user community.

extreme_events.evidence: Can detect fire and drought impacts on carbon cycle. Limited resolution for individual extreme events.

obs_coverage.evidence: Multiple satellite and in-situ observation types assimilated.

error_correction.evidence: Bayesian framework naturally accounts for model and observation errors.

prediction_impact.evidence: DA significantly constrains parameter and state uncertainties relative to prior.

operational_use.evidence: Not used in any operational system.

verification_level.evidence: Code verification through synthetic experiments and comparison with analytical solutions.

da_readiness.evidence: Can incorporate new satellite data but requires significant adaptation.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Supports terrestrial ecology priorities but not directly tied to specific missions.

operational_usability.evidence: Research tool only. Not packaged for operational use.

regulatory_relevance.evidence: Potential for forest carbon monitoring and REDD+ verification.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Full Bayesian uncertainty quantification. Posterior distributions communicate uncertainty naturally.

community_training.evidence: Academic training through collaborations. Limited broader outreach.

process_representation.evidence: Multi-model chemical data assimilation using ensemble of chemistry transport models. Represents tropospheric chemistry, aerosols, and trace gas transport.

spatial_resolution.evidence: Global coverage at ~1° to 2° resolution. Captures regional-scale pollution features.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Daily to monthly chemical reanalysis products. Multi-year records spanning satellite era.

process_coupling.evidence: Multi-model ensemble approach implicitly represents model structural uncertainty. Off-line coupling to meteorological fields.

predictive_skill.evidence: Demonstrated skill in tropospheric composition analysis. Validated against independent observations and other reanalyses.

computational_performance.evidence: Multi-model ensemble requires running several CTMs. Computationally expensive. Limited HPC optimization.

observational_constraint.evidence: Assimilates satellite retrievals of ozone, CO, NO2, and aerosol from multiple instruments (OMI, MOPITT, MODIS).

retrospective_analysis.evidence: Multi-year chemical reanalysis products. Used for trend analysis and air quality assessment.

uq_attribution.evidence: Multi-model spread provides structural uncertainty estimate. Ensemble Kalman filter provides formal UQ.

vv_framework.evidence: Validated against independent observations (AERONET, ozonesondes, aircraft campaigns). Intercomparison with CAMS reanalysis.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: No ML/AI integration in current framework.

mission_support.evidence: Supports interpretation of satellite atmospheric composition observations. Limited direct mission formulation role.

interoperability.evidence: Code shared with JAMSTEC collaborators. Not publicly available as open source.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Products used in research community. Limited operational or decision support adoption.

process_fidelity.evidence: Multi-model approach captures range of chemistry representations.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: Off-line coupling. No interactive atmosphere-chemistry feedback.

community_recognition.evidence: Growing recognition. Smaller citation base than established reanalyses.

extreme_events.evidence: Can detect major pollution events and wildfire plumes at resolved scales.

obs_coverage.evidence: Multiple satellite observations of atmospheric composition assimilated.

error_correction.evidence: Ensemble approach provides adaptive error correction. Multi-model framework reduces structural bias.

prediction_impact.evidence: DA significantly improves chemical composition estimates relative to free-running models.

operational_use.evidence: Not used in operational systems. Research product.

verification_level.evidence: Individual CTMs have their own verification. System-level verification limited.

independent_validation.evidence: Validated against independent surface and aircraft observations.

da_readiness.evidence: Can incorporate new satellite data but requires development.

regulatory_relevance.evidence: Potential for air quality monitoring. Not yet used in regulatory context.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Multi-model spread provides uncertainty information. Limited formal communication.

process_representation.evidence: Explicitly resolves turbulent eddies and cloud microphysics. State-of-the-art representation of boundary layer dynamics, convection, and precipitation processes.

spatial_resolution.evidence: Meter-scale resolution resolving the inertial subrange of turbulence. Sub-cloud to cloud-resolving scales for diverse atmospheric phenomena.

temporal_resolution.evidence: Sub-second time stepping for turbulence dynamics. Limited to short simulation periods (hours to days) due to computational cost.

process_coupling.evidence: Standalone atmospheric process model. Driven by prescribed boundary conditions. No coupling to larger-scale models in production.

predictive_skill.evidence: High skill for process-level studies. Not used for operational weather prediction. Provides truth-proxy for parameterization development.

computational_performance.evidence: Highly parallel with demonstrated GPU acceleration. Scalable on HPC systems for large-domain simulations.

observational_constraint.evidence: Validated against field campaign observations and satellite retrievals. No formal data assimilation.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: No retrospective analysis or reanalysis capability. Process-study focused.

uq_attribution.evidence: Ensemble simulations for sensitivity studies. No formal probabilistic UQ framework.

vv_framework.evidence: Validated against field campaigns (DYCOMS, BOMEX, ARM) and DNS solutions. Benchmarked in LES intercomparisons.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: Active ML research for developing cloud and turbulence parameterizations from LES output. ML emulators being developed for GCM application.

mission_support.evidence: Provides process understanding for atmospheric observation missions. Limited direct mission formulation role.

interoperability.evidence: Code shared within research group. Not publicly open source. Limited external access.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Research tool only. No operational or decision support applications.

process_fidelity.evidence: Explicitly resolves turbulent processes that are parameterized in GCMs. Highest fidelity atmospheric process model.

community_recognition.evidence: Respected in atmospheric science community. LES output used for parameterization development worldwide.

extreme_events.evidence: Can simulate extreme precipitation and convective events at high fidelity.

verification_level.evidence: Verified against DNS and analytical solutions for idealized cases.

ml_in_processes.evidence: ML trained on LES output for parameterization development. Active research area.

vvuq_ml_emulators.evidence: Some validation of ML-derived parameterizations against LES reference simulations.

independent_validation.evidence: Validated against field campaigns and satellite observations.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Supports cloud and precipitation process understanding for future missions.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Ensemble simulations provide uncertainty information for process studies.

process_representation.evidence: Unified parameterization scheme combining eddy diffusivity and mass flux for boundary layer turbulence and shallow convection. Bridges gap between LES and GCM scales.

spatial_resolution.evidence: Designed for GCM grid scales (~25-100 km). Represents sub-grid processes through parameterization. Not a resolved-scale model.

temporal_resolution.evidence: GCM time stepping (minutes to hours). Parameterizes processes across relevant timescales.

process_coupling.evidence: Designed to couple with GCM dynamical cores. Tested in GFDL AM4, NCAR CAM, and NASA GEOS. Represents coupling between turbulence and convection.

predictive_skill.evidence: Improves GCM cloud and precipitation representation relative to traditional parameterizations. Demonstrated in limited case studies.

computational_performance.evidence: Computationally efficient for GCM integration. Comparable cost to traditional parameterizations. Scalable through GCM infrastructure.

observational_constraint.evidence: Parameterization parameters tuned against LES output and field campaign observations. No formal data assimilation.

retrospective_analysis.evidence: No standalone reanalysis capability. Contributes through GCM integration.

uq_attribution.evidence: Bayesian calibration framework for parameter uncertainty. Systematic sensitivity analysis to LES training data.

vv_framework.evidence: Validated against LES reference simulations, field campaigns, and within GCM climate simulations. Participates in CFMIP experiments.

ml_ai_integration.evidence: Active research on ML-based calibration of EDMF parameters using LES data. ML used for parameter optimization and uncertainty quantification.

mission_support.evidence: No direct mission support. Improves GCM cloud representation relevant to Earth observation missions.

interoperability.evidence: Integrated with multiple GCMs (GFDL, NCAR, NASA GSFC). Code shared with collaborators.

stakeholder_adoption.evidence: Research parameterization. No direct stakeholder applications.

process_fidelity.evidence: Improved representation of boundary layer and shallow convection relative to traditional schemes.

coupling_fidelity.evidence: Well-coupled with GCM dynamics. Tested in multiple modeling frameworks.

community_recognition.evidence: Growing recognition in atmospheric modeling community. Cited in parameterization development literature.

extreme_events.evidence: Improved representation of convective events in GCMs. Limited assessment of extreme event performance.

obs_coverage.evidence: No DA capability as standalone parameterization.

prediction_impact.evidence: Improves GCM forecasts when integrated but not independently evaluated.

verification_level.evidence: Verified against LES and analytical solutions for canonical cases.

ml_in_processes.evidence: ML calibration of parameterization parameters. Active research area.

vvuq_ml_emulators.evidence: Some validation of ML-calibrated parameters against LES reference.

independent_validation.evidence: Validated against LES, field campaigns, and satellite cloud observations.

decadal_alignment.evidence: Supports cloud and climate priorities indirectly through GCM improvement.

uncertainty_communication.evidence: Bayesian parameter uncertainty provides some uncertainty information.
